title: pleurospermum rubellum
intro: Pleurospermum rubellum, also known as New Zealand celery or mountain celery, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic, helping to reduce fluid retention and alleviate symptoms associated with urinary tract issues. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in herbal preparations to support kidney function and alleviate pain, with potential health benefits including improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune system function. In traditional herbalism, Pleurospermum rubellum is often prepared as a tea or tincture, with some practitioners also using it in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and reduce pain.
